config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because that is where abuse and oppression often begin. In my view, a society is healthiest when it protects personal freedoms while still setting fair limits for the common good. If the balance is wrong, the collective can easily silence the vulnerable in the name of “society.”

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum, karena menurut saya di Amerika kita punya pelajaran panjang bahwa “demi kebaikan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi seharusnya menjadi batas dasar yang tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya karena mayoritas menginginkannya. Kepentingan sosial penting, namun harus dicapai lewat pembatasan yang sempit, adil, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dengan menempatkan orang sebagai alat bagi kolektif. Kalau perlindungan hak pribadi melemah, yang paling cepat terdampak biasanya justru kelompok minoritas dan yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because in Indonesia that kind of logic can too easily become permission for authorities or majorities to silence dissent and protect the powerful. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are a license to ignore public welfare, since our communal culture shows that people do have obligations to each other. The real issue is not choosing one side absolutely, but making sure any limits on rights are narrow, fair, and truly necessary. If “society” is used as a vague excuse, then the people who lose first are usually the weak, not the selfish.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena dalam pengalaman Amerika, frasa itu terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan publik memang nyata, tetapi kalau hak dasar bisa dikorbankan hanya karena mayoritas merasa itu berguna, maka minoritas dan orang rentan akan selalu paling mudah dirugikan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan membatasi negara dan mayoritas agar tidak melampaui batas yang melindungi kebebasan pribadi. Jadi, kepentingan sosial penting, tetapi hanya sah jika dicapai lewat cara yang sempit, adil, dan benar-benar perlu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should automatically take priority over individual rights, because in Indonesia that idea is too easily used to justify pressure, conformity, and abuse by authorities or majorities. Our communal culture does teach responsibility to others, but that does not mean a vague “common good” should be allowed to override basic freedoms whenever it is convenient. I also think the recent point about narrow and accountable limits is exactly right: society can set rules, but those rules must be truly necessary and not become a cover for silencing the weak. If we lose individual rights first, the people most harmed are usually not the powerful, but ordinary citizens and minorities.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum, karena dari sudut pandang Amerika, itu terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran bagi penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi kalau hak dasar bisa disisihkan setiap kali mayoritas merasa itu berguna, maka minoritas dan orang rentan yang akan paling cepat dirugikan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menelan individu demi kolektif, melainkan yang membatasi negara dan mayoritas agar tidak melampaui hak yang harus tetap jadi garis merah. Pembatasan hak hanya sah jika sempit, adil, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan sekadar karena terdengar baik atas nama “masyarakat.”

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
